hooks.command: cmd=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if printf "%s" "$cmd" | grep -qE "\b(grep|egrep|rg|ripgrep)\b"; then jq -n "{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:\"PreToolUse\",additionalContext:\"cx-nudge: this Bash call uses grep/rg. For code navigation (symbol definitions, references, callers) prefer the cx tool (see the cx skill) per CLAUDE.md. If this is a plain-text search cx cannot do, ignore this.\"}}"; fi

jq -r 'def comments: . / "\n" | map(select(test("(^|[^:])//|/\\*|^\\s*\\*")));
(.tool_input.new_string // .tool_input.content // "") as $new
| (.tool_input.old_string // "") as $old
| ($new | comments) as $nc
| ($old | comments) as $oc
| ($nc - $oc) as $added
| if ($added | length) > 0 then
    ($added | any(test("\\b(now|also|instead|no longer|previously|used to|this (change|edit|fix))\\b"; "i"))) as $narr
    | ($added | map("  > " + .) | join("\n")) as $lines
    | (if $narr then "⚠ comment-audit — added comment line(s) that look like they NARRATE the change:\n" else "comment-audit — you added these comment line(s):\n" end
        + $lines
        + "\n\nBefore anything else, quote each line and rule on it: would a reader with ZERO knowledge of this conversation be UTTERLY CONFUSED without it? Delete it if it explains what changed, justifies the edit, uses change-relative words (now / also / instead / no longer / previously / used to), or restates the code. Narrating the diff is the default failure — treat each line as guilty until justified.") as $msg
    | {hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PostToolUse", additionalContext: $msg}}
  else empty end
'
